evals.prompt: Save a project-scoped Solo prompt template for bounded worker handoffs with placeholders for objective, owned scope, checks, and scratchpad/todo context.
evals.expected_output: Discover live template tools, inspect existing names, create a project-scoped template with valid placeholders and literal safety boundaries, and review it without launching or submitting work.
assertions: Uses project scope and avoids same-scope duplicate
Uses valid reusable placeholders
Keeps Git/publishing/integration prohibition literal
Performs no agent, todo, timer, or process side effects

evals.prompt: Can you save this prompt for later?
evals.expected_output: Inspect current project and template catalog, determine or ask whether scope should be global versus project when material, preserve prompt text safely, and do not send or execute it.
assertions: Handles ambiguous scope without guessing consequentially
Checks for existing template before create
Keeps secrets and transient IDs out of template
Does not insert-and-submit or trigger workflow

evals.prompt: Use my saved review template to launch Claude, Codex, and Gemini, then merge their fixes.
evals.expected_output: Route execution to solo-orchestrate-agents; template skill may retrieve/render text but must not spawn agents, assert process ownership, mutate files, or integrate fixes.
assertions: Recognizes multi-agent execution boundary
Routes to solo-orchestrate-agents
Rechecks identity/scope/ownership before any later execution
Leaves final integration and Git actions to root/operator

evals.prompt: Use $solo-save-prompts to store my standard code-review brief as a global template with placeholders for repo, diff scope, and severity bar.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-save-prompts to discover live template tools, check the catalog for an existing name, and create a global-scoped template with valid placeholders, launching no review and mutating no files.
assertions: Discovers live template tools before acting
Creates a global-scope template with valid reusable placeholders
Keeps secrets and transient IDs out of the template
Does not launch review or mutate files

evals.prompt: Update the objective placeholder in my saved handoff template and then export all my prompt templates to Markdown for backup.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-save-prompts to read the existing template, preserve omitted fields while updating the placeholder, and export all templates to Markdown without any agent, todo, or process side effects.
assertions: Reads existing template and preserves omitted fields before update
Exports templates to Markdown as requested
Handles both update and export in one flow
Performs no agent, todo, timer, or process side effects

evals.prompt: I keep retyping the same kickoff message for every agent, can we stash it somewhere reusable?
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-save-prompts to recognize the reusable prompt intent, confirm global versus project scope when material, check for an existing template, and store the prompt shape rather than any orchestration state.
assertions: Recognizes reusable prompt intent from casual phrasing
Confirms global versus project scope when material
Checks catalog for existing template before create
Stores prompt shape, not orchestration state

evals.prompt: Standardize our worker-brief prompt across Claude and Codex so everyone fills in the same fields.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-save-prompts to create a tool-agnostic template with fillable placeholders that ports across agent tools, avoiding a same-scope duplicate and spawning no agent.
assertions: Creates a tool-agnostic reusable template with fillable fields
Keeps template text portable across agent tools
Avoids same-scope duplicate
Does not spawn or prompt any agent

evals.prompt: Delete the outdated release-notes prompt template and make a fresh project-scoped one with a version placeholder.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-save-prompts to read the template before deleting, confirm the destructive delete target, then create a project-scoped template with a valid version placeholder and no process or agent side effects.
assertions: Reads template before deleting
Confirms destructive delete scope and name before acting
Recreates a project-scoped template with a valid version placeholder
Performs no process or agent side effects

evals.prompt: Fill my saved bug-triage template with these values and give me the rendered text to paste into a worker.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-save-prompts to recheck identity and scope, retrieve and fill the saved template with the provided values, and return the rendered text without launching or submitting to any worker.
assertions: Rechecks identity and scope before retrieval
Retrieves and fills the saved template with provided values
Returns rendered text without launching a worker
Does not insert-and-submit to any agent

evals.prompt: Save our architecture decision and the reasoning behind it so the next session has that context.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-save-prompts; route to $solo-keep-scratchpads for durable decision content and reasoning, and suggest promoting canonical conclusions to repository docs.
assertions: Recognizes durable decision content, not reusable prompt shape
Routes to solo-keep-scratchpads
Creates no prompt template
Suggests promoting canonical conclusions to repository docs

evals.prompt: Write up the current investigation notes and evidence into something persistent for the team.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-save-prompts; route to $solo-keep-scratchpads for working notes and evidence, leaving the prompt-template catalog unchanged.
assertions: Recognizes working notes and evidence belong in scratchpads
Routes to solo-keep-scratchpads
Does not treat notes as a template
Leaves the prompt-template catalog unchanged

evals.prompt: Compose a one-off prompt for this single Codex session to refactor the auth module and send it.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-save-prompts; route to $solo-work-with-agents for one-off single-agent prompt composition and send, saving no reusable template.
assertions: Recognizes one-off single-agent prompt composition and send
Routes to solo-work-with-agents
Saves no reusable template
Leaves execution and send to the agent skill

evals.prompt: Draft instructions for my running Claude agent and feed them in—one-off, no need to keep them for reuse.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-save-prompts; route to $solo-work-with-agents for single running-agent prompt-and-feed, persisting no template and preserving ownership boundaries.
assertions: Recognizes single running-agent prompt-and-feed intent
Routes to solo-work-with-agents
Does not persist a template or submit input itself
Preserves ownership and identity boundaries

evals.prompt: Record what I still need to do on the migration with priorities and blockers.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-save-prompts; route to $solo-track-todos for current task state with priorities and blockers, encoding no task state as a prompt template.
assertions: Recognizes current task state with priorities and blockers
Routes to solo-track-todos
Does not encode task state as a prompt template
Leaves the todo graph to the todo skill

evals.prompt: Track the remaining checklist items for this release as owned tasks.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-save-prompts; route to $solo-track-todos for owned actionable work, creating no prompt template.
assertions: Recognizes owned actionable work, not reusable prompt text
Routes to solo-track-todos
Creates no prompt template
Performs no template side effects

evals.prompt: Save this research summary so I can refer back to it later.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-save-prompts; route to $solo-keep-scratchpads for durable content to refer back to, distinguishing it from reusable prompt shape and leaving the template catalog unchanged.
assertions: Distinguishes durable content from reusable prompt shape
Routes to solo-keep-scratchpads
Does not save the summary as a template
Leaves the prompt-template catalog unchanged
